title: Tetrastigma tetragynum
intro: Tetrastigma tetragynum, also known as Thai mulberry or Chinese grapevine, is a tropical vine species native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal problems, thanks to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tetrastigma tetragynum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Tetrastigma tetragynum is often used in herbal preparations such as tea, infusion, and extract, and is commonly used in traditional medicine in countries like China, Thailand, and Vietnam.
taxonomy: Tetrastigma tetragynum is classified under the family Vitaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as grape or wine vine family. This species belongs to the genus Tetrastigma, which includes a variety of species found in tropical Asia. As a member of the family Vitaceae, Tetrastigma tetragynum is closely related to other grape and wine vine species. The family Vitaceae is also part of the order Vitales, which is a small order of flowering plants. Within the order Vitales, the family Vitaceae is one of the few families that are primarily composed of woody vines.
